seoTitle: Filter a Confluence Table: 2026 Quick Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to filter a Confluence table in 2026 using inline filters on published pages, or advanced multi-column rules inside a Table Filter macro.
intro: Scrolling through an endless data grid to find one row wastes time. This guide shows how to filter a Confluence table in 2026, from a simple column filter on a live page to layered rules for more complex reports.

steps.heading: Confirm your table is published
steps.body: Open your target page and verify your main data grid layout is fully published.

steps.heading: Use the inline filter
steps.body: Hover over the table header and click the native funnel or filter icon.

steps.heading: Set a search string
steps.body: Select your target column and type a search string to hide mismatched rows instantly.

steps.heading: Use advanced filter macros
steps.body: For more complex grids, open the page in edit mode and use `/table filter` to drop an interactive control box.

steps.heading: Layer multiple rules
steps.body: Toggle checkboxes, text matching rules, and custom value ranges, layering multiple column rules to isolate high-priority rows.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Click Publish to lock down the interactive filtered layout for your workspace.

faq.question: Can I filter a table without editing the page?
faq.answer: Yes, hovering over the header and clicking the funnel icon lets you filter directly on the published view.

faq.question: Can I filter by more than one column at once?
faq.answer: Yes, layering multiple column rules simultaneously lets you isolate rows that match several conditions at once.

faq.question: Will filtering delete or modify my data?
faq.answer: No, filtering only hides mismatched rows temporarily without modifying or dropping the underlying data.

faq.question: Can I save a default filtered view for my team?
faq.answer: Yes, saving a pre-filtered configuration in edit mode lets your team open an already-optimized data view.
